response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled full-stack and web engineer with broad polyglot experience, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. His C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a university thesis project — far below the mandatory 5–10 years of professional C++ on Windows. There is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle expertise, no capital markets or fixed-income domain knowledge, and the financial-services exposure at RIDE Capital was limited to general investment tooling using Node.js and TypeScript rather than trading or fixed-income systems. The optional desiderata (Summit, STL, Kafka, Java in a financial context) are also absent.
strengths: Demonstrated CI/CD and DevOps experience (GitLab, Docker, Artifactory, trunk-based development) aligns with desirable pipeline skills
Genuine financial-services adjacency through RIDE Capital engagement, showing some ability to operate in regulated or finance-adjacent environments
Strong communication track record evidenced by conference talks, full-day workshops, and open-source community contributions
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months professionally, one thesis project) versus the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ that is explicitly mandatory
No Fixed Income, Fixed Income Derivatives, Investment Banking, or Capital Markets systems experience of any kind
No evidence of SQL Server or Oracle proficiency; SQL appears only as a bullet in a skills list with no supporting project detail
